title: anagallis arvensis subsp. parviflora
intro: Anagallis arvensis subsp. parviflora is a subspecies of the Anagallis arvensis plant, commonly known as the blue pimpernel or red pimpernel, a low-growing, annual or biennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. The medicinal properties of Anagallis arvensis subsp. parviflora include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. Anagallis arvensis subsp. parviflora can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
